topic: Why do we need to sleep? What happens in your brain
narration: Every night, your brain does something no machine can replicate. While you sleep, it flushes out toxic proteins that build up during the day, ones linked to memory loss and disease. It replays your day's memories, deciding what to keep and what to let go. Your immune system ramps up, repairing tissue and fighting infection. Skip sleep, and none of this happens properly. That's not laziness catching up with you, that's your brain literally not getting the maintenance it needs to function. Sleep isn't rest. It's repair.
caption:
We treat sleep like a luxury we can skip. But your brain treats it like maintenance it can't skip.

Every night your brain clears out waste proteins, consolidates memories, and repairs your immune system. Chronic sleep loss doesn't just make you tired, it interferes with all three.

Swipe through, save this, and protect your 7-8 hours like the medical necessity it is.
